publication_policy.reason: Research evidence only. Facts must be converted to the practical-guide schema, reviewed, and pass fail-closed publication validation before release.
publication_policy.source_rule: Only the named government bodies, statutory authorities, municipalities, regulators, NS, 9292 and UWV were used. No secondary editorial source was used as evidence.
publication_policy.fact_rule: Every supported fact has one or more source_ids. Gaps are explicit and must not be filled with inferred or AI-generated instructions.

dossiers.title: Finding a huisarts
dossiers.scope: National orientation for residents seeking a regular GP; practice acceptance and local availability remain provider- and region-specific.

facts.statement: Registering with a family doctor is not mandatory, but the Government of the Netherlands recommends doing so so that care is accessible when needed.
The Government of the Netherlands directs people who need help finding a family doctor to their municipality or health insurer.
Zorginstituut Nederland says a GP has a guideline to be able to reach a patient for a home visit within 15 minutes and advises choosing a nearby practice.
facts.caveat: This is a guideline described by Zorginstituut, not a guarantee that a particular practice accepts a patient.

facts.statement: When a provider has an excessive wait or announces a patient stop, patients can ask their health insurer for care mediation; the provider must point patients to this option in the situations identified by the NZa.

GP care and practice support are covered by the basic package and GP care itself is not subject to the compulsory deductible; prescribed medicines, tests performed outside the practice and referred care may be subject to the deductible.
facts.caveat: The insurer's policy conditions determine provider and reimbursement details.

sources.title: What do I need to arrange if I’m moving to the Netherlands?

gaps: The permitted official sources do not provide one national registration form, universal document list, acceptance radius, processing time or guarantee that a practice has space.
A publishable guide needs a locality-aware method for finding actual practices and must make clear that availability and registration requirements are set by the practice and insurer.
No practice directory has been accepted into this dossier because the requested source set excludes commercial directories and provider-maintained listings.

dossiers.title: Dutch health insurance
dossiers.scope: Basic Dutch health insurance for people who live or work in the Netherlands, with explicit exceptions and status-dependent rules.

facts.statement: A person who comes to live or work in the Netherlands and is subject to the Dutch insurance duty must arrange Dutch health insurance as soon as possible and no later than four months after arrival, with coverage from the date of arrival.
facts.caveat: The same source identifies exceptions, including some foreign-employer, EU-institution, student and asylum situations; the duty must not be stated without those qualifications.

facts.statement: The standard health insurance package is compulsory for people subject to the Dutch insurance duty; supplementary insurance is optional and is arranged with an insurer.
For 2026 the compulsory deductible is €385 for insured people aged 18 or over, while listed care including GP and out-of-hours GP care is exempt from the compulsory deductible.
facts.caveat: The amount is calendar-year specific and must be reverified for later years. A personal contribution is a separate concept.

facts.statement: After a CAK uninsured letter, the recipient must either take out Dutch basic insurance if required or request an SVB Wlz assessment if they believe the duty does not apply; doing neither within three months leads to a CAK fine.
The CAK page states that the uninsured fine is €529.74 in 2026 and that this amount is indexed annually.
facts.caveat: Highly volatile annual amount; never carry it into a later year without reverification.

sources.title: I am coming to live or work in the Netherlands. When do I need to take out compulsory health insurance?
I received a letter that I'm uninsured

gaps: No single rule covers every international student, cross-border worker, foreign employer, asylum seeker or residence-status scenario; these branches require separate reviewed source mappings.
Premiums, contracted providers, reimbursements and voluntary deductible choices are insurer- and policy-specific; no fixed price or best-policy recommendation is supported here.
Healthcare benefit eligibility is not included because it requires a separate Tax Administration evidence dossier.

dossiers.title: Emergency numbers and urgent help
dossiers.scope: National emergency calling, urgent primary-care routing and police contact distinctions.

facts.statement: Call 112 when urgent help is needed because life is in immediate danger, someone is seriously injured and needs prompt medical attention, or a serious crime is being witnessed.
When calling 112, say what happened, what help is needed and whether police, fire brigade or ambulance appears to be required.
If the regular GP practice is closed, contact the out-of-hours GP service before going to a hospital emergency department, except in a life-threatening situation, when 112 should be called directly.
GP and out-of-hours GP care do not consume the compulsory deductible; hospital emergency-department care is hospital care and is subject to the compulsory deductible.
For police emergencies in the Netherlands use 112 free of charge; for non-emergency police questions or appointments use 0900-8844, and from abroad use +31 343 57 8844.
facts.caveat: 0900-8844 is a police number, not a general medical advice line.

sources.title: Wanneer moet ik naar de huisartsenpost of de spoedeisende hulppost?

gaps: Out-of-hours GP phone numbers are regional and are not supplied by the national sources in this dossier; a location-aware contact source is required.
This dossier does not provide symptom-by-symptom triage and must not be used to infer medical urgency beyond the official routing statements.
Other crisis lines, poison information, domestic violence and mental-health crisis contacts require separate primary-source verification before inclusion.

dossiers.title: Renting a home
dossiers.scope: National tenant checks with municipality-dependent permit and enforcement branches.

facts.statement: Before accepting a rental, determine whether it is social, midprice or private-sector housing because different rules apply, and use the Huurcommissie Rent Check that matches the accommodation type.
facts.caveat: Rent thresholds and point values are year-specific and must be reverified when displayed.

facts.statement: For tenancy agreements dated 1 July 2023 or later, the maximum deposit described by Government.nl is two months' basic rent.

Tenancy agreements dated 1 July 2023 or later must be in writing, and the landlord must provide specified written information about use, access, protection, repairs, contacts, deposit and service costs.

A tenancy agreement should distinguish basic rent from service costs and record the parties, address, property description, start date, payment terms, deposit and signatures; the property's condition should be recorded in writing at the start.
An agent acting for the landlord may not also charge the tenant a mediation fee; a tenant who independently instructs an agent to find a home may owe that agent's fee.
Some municipalities require landlords to have a rental permit and can attach conditions, so the municipality must be checked for the specific address.

sources.title: Welke afspraken staan er in het huurcontract van mijn woning?

gaps: No official source in the permitted set provides a complete national inventory of available rentals or verifies individual listings and landlords.
Municipal housing permits, allocation rules and local reporting routes require city-specific sources before publication.
Annual rent thresholds, points and benefit eligibility are volatile; this dossier intentionally does not turn 2026 figures into evergreen copy.

dossiers.title: Reporting housing defects
dossiers.scope: Evidence-preserving first report to a landlord and sector-dependent escalation to Huurcommissie, municipality or court.

facts.statement: First report poor maintenance to the landlord by email or letter, list the defects, ask for repair within six weeks and keep a copy; request urgency when the problem is urgent.

For the Huurcommissie maintenance-defect procedure, the landlord has six weeks after notification to remedy the defects; if they do not, the tenant can submit a request and must provide dated evidence of the notification.

Huurcommissie authority differs by rental sector and contract date; for a private-sector home it can provide only advice when both parties agreed in writing to submit the dispute, while social and qualifying midprice cases can receive decisions under the stated rules.
facts.caveat: The sector and transition rules are legally detailed and should be determined before presenting a specific route.

facts.statement: A tenant can also report poor maintenance to the municipality; if the municipality finds the home violates the Building Works Living Environment Decree, it can order the landlord to repair and can arrange the work at the landlord's cost if the order is ignored.

Every municipality has a reporting point for unwanted landlord conduct under the Good Landlordship Act, including missing written agreements or information, unjustified service or deposit costs, discrimination and intimidation.
facts.caveat: This reporting point is not automatically the correct enforcement route for every physical repair defect.

sources.title: Hoe zorg ik ervoor dat de verhuurder slecht onderhoud aanpakt?
Gemeentelijk meldpunt goed verhuurderschap
sources.publisher: Volkshuisvesting Nederland, Ministry of the Interior and Kingdom Relations

gaps: The correct escalation route depends on sector, contract date, defect type and municipality; these must be collected before generating personalised steps.
The dossier does not define emergency responses for gas leaks, fire, structural collapse or immediate health danger; dedicated official safety sources are required.
Court procedure, limitation periods and legal drafting are not expanded into advice here.

dossiers.title: Using public transport
dossiers.scope: Planning a multimodal journey and basic contactless check-in/check-out behavior, without fixing fares or product recommendations.

facts.statement: 9292 can plan public-transport journeys and shows planned disruptions; because timetables and disruptions can change, its guidance says to check the route shortly before departure for the correct date and time.
The 9292 planner has an accessible-journey option that can add transfer time and route via stops and stations marked accessible.
facts.caveat: This is planner behavior, not a guarantee that every facility is operational at travel time.

facts.statement: For occasional travel, NS states that a valid contactless debit card, credit card or mobile device can be used without prior activation; the same card or device must be used to check out.
Check out with the operator used to check in; a change to another train operator can require checking out and checking in again.
NS describes debit-card travel as second class without a discount and advises season-ticket holders to continue using the product and card associated with their season ticket.

sources.title: Frequently asked questions about the 9292 travel planner
Check in and out with your debit card

gaps: No fare is included because fares, supplements, discounts and products vary by operator, journey and date.
NS evidence is strongest for train travel; bus, tram, metro and ferry operator exceptions need their own primary-source checks before a comprehensive national guide is published.
A route planner result is advisory and can change; YouNew must not cache it as evergreen operational truth.

dossiers.title: Finding work
dossiers.scope: UWV/EURES routes and secure UWV tools, clearly separating ordinary jobseekers from unemployment-benefit obligations.

facts.statement: EURES helps jobseekers find work in EU/EEA countries and Switzerland, and UWV is the coordinating office for EURES in the Netherlands.
UWV's Work Folder can be used to post a CV, search vacancies and access courses or webinars; benefit recipients can also report job-search activities there.
facts.caveat: Access requires the appropriate DigiD or recognised European electronic identity route described by UWV.

facts.statement: A person who comes to the Netherlands to seek work while exporting an unemployment benefit from another EU/EEA country or Switzerland must obtain PD U2 from the foreign benefit body and register as a jobseeker with UWV by telephone within seven days.
facts.caveat: This fact applies only to the PD U2 benefit-export scenario, not to every jobseeker.

facts.statement: For people who are subject to UWV's unemployment-benefit job-search requirement, recognised activities include applications, interviews, posting a CV, registering with an agency, employer contact, networking and specified workshops, and evidence should be retained for up to two years.
facts.caveat: These obligations are for applicable benefit recipients and must never be presented as a legal duty for all jobseekers.

sources.title: How to use and access our UWV portals

gaps: The permitted sources do not establish one universal job-search sequence, response time or outcome guarantee.
Work-permit requirements depend on nationality, residence status and job; this dossier does not contain enough primary evidence to publish those branches.
Commercial job boards, recruitment agencies and salary advice are intentionally excluded.

dossiers.title: Understanding an employment contract
dossiers.scope: Core information duties and high-level distinctions; not individual contract interpretation or legal advice.

facts.statement: Within one week after employment starts, the employer must provide specified information in writing, including the parties, workplace, role, start date, fixed-term duration where relevant, working hours, salary/payment timing and any probation length.

Within one month after employment starts, the employer must provide further specified written information, including holiday allowance, holidays, notice period and any applicable pension, non-compete clause and collective agreement.
An employment contract can be oral or written, but Rijksoverheid advises recording it in writing so rights and duties are fixed clearly.

A fixed-term contract normally ends automatically at its end or project completion and usually cannot be ended early unless this was agreed; a permanent contract continues until employee or employer ends it under the applicable rules.

An applicable collective labour agreement can govern pay and other terms and takes priority over an individual contract; the individual contract may not be less favourable than the collective agreement, while more favourable terms can be allowed.

sources.title: Wat staat er in een arbeidsovereenkomst?
Wat is het verschil tussen een tijdelijk contract en een vast contract?

gaps: No individual contract, collective agreement or sector has been reviewed; publication must not label a clause lawful or unlawful for a specific person.
Probation, dismissal, notice, non-compete, agency work and zero-hours rules need their own sourced branches before deeper guidance.
Legislative news about future flex-work changes is not treated as law in force in this dossier.

dossiers.title: Opening a Dutch bank account
dossiers.scope: Consumer payment accounts, KYC and basic-account fallback; not bank-specific onboarding.

facts.statement: A bank decides whether to accept an application for an ordinary payment account and may ask about identity and the source of funds because it must prevent money laundering and terrorist financing.

A bank must verify the customer's identity and may make or request a copy of an identity document; it may request other personal data, including a BSN when needed to meet tax-law obligations, and must explain why data is needed.
facts.caveat: The DNB source does not say that every ordinary account application universally requires a BSN.

facts.statement: A consumer who cannot open an ordinary payment account can apply for a basic payment account, subject to statutory refusal grounds including KYC compliance and proof of a genuine connection to the Netherlands such as living, studying or working here.
A consumer who disagrees with a rejection can complain to the bank and, if dissatisfied with the response, may in many cases complain to Kifid or go to court.

sources.title: Wat kan ik als consument doen als ik geen betaalrekening kan krijgen?

gaps: The official sources do not provide one universal document checklist, opening time, fee or remote-onboarding route for all banks.
The evidence does not support the claim that a BSN is always required to open every ordinary Dutch account.
Business accounts, credit products, savings accounts and bank comparisons are outside this dossier.

dossiers.title: Studying in the Netherlands
dossiers.scope: Higher-education recognition, general application deadlines, tuition categories and conditional student-finance orientation.

facts.statement: Before enrolling, verify that the institution is recognised by Dutch authorities and that the bachelor's or master's programme is accredited by NVAO.

For a first associate-degree or bachelor's application, the general deadline is 1 May via Studielink; a numerus-fixus programme has a 15 January deadline, while master's deadlines must be checked with the programme.
facts.caveat: Some programmes permit later application and the institution decides admission; the institution's current deadline and requirements must always be checked.

facts.statement: The university or university of applied sciences decides admission, and conditions can differ by institution and by programme.

Higher education has statutory and institutional tuition; the statutory tuition fee for academic year 2026-2027 is €2,694, while institutions set institutional tuition and eligibility for the statutory fee depends on the official criteria.
facts.caveat: The amount is academic-year specific and must be reverified for another year.

facts.statement: Enrolled students may apply for student finance only if they meet nationality or equivalent-rights and other eligibility conditions; its components can include grants, a travel product and loans, and student finance is not always a gift.
facts.caveat: Being an international student does not by itself establish eligibility.

sources.title: Wanneer moet ik me aanmelden voor een opleiding aan de universiteit of hogeschool?

gaps: A complete guide needs separate, verified branches for MBO, HBO, WO, bachelor, master, numerus fixus and February entry.
Residence permits, visas, foreign-diploma evaluation, language tests and institution-specific documents are not fully sourced in this dossier.
The dossier does not claim that every international student is eligible for statutory tuition or student finance.

dossiers.title: Student housing
dossiers.scope: National tenant safeguards and broad search routes; supply, registration and permits remain local.

facts.statement: Rijksoverheid identifies housing associations that offer student rooms and private landlords, advertisements, agents and personal networks as possible room-search routes.
facts.caveat: These are routes to search, not verified listings or a guarantee of availability.

facts.statement: After finding a room, the tenant should have a tenancy agreement recording rent, termination and other agreements and must report the new address to the municipality on time.
A housing permit may be required for a room depending on the situation and municipality.
Shared accommodation is treated as social housing with a rent ceiling in the Government.nl tenant plan, and the Huurcommissie offers a Rent Check specifically for shared accommodation.
Student rooms remain subject to the ordinary tenant checks for written agreements, deposit, service costs and landlord conduct described in the national tenant plan.

sources.title: Op kamers: wat moet ik regelen?
Studeren: check wat je moet regelen

gaps: There is no official national live inventory, universal waiting time or guaranteed student-housing route in the permitted source set.
Housing-association registration, campus housing, local allocation, housing permits and address-registration rules require city and institution sources.
Housing-benefit eligibility for a particular room is not established by this dossier.
